metadata.title: Claude, Steered
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream; code stays Onda dark glass
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats pair the spark with one short serif line; typing is B00-only. OUTRO LAW: outro restates the title. Spine: B00 ASK · B01 THE-STACK (code: three-layer system message concept) · B02 THREE-LAYERS (method/register/brand) · B03 STACKING-WORKS (each layer narrows) · B04 THE-LIMIT (steered not specified, callback to E7) · B05 ONE-PIPELINE (four channels, one belt) · B06 PARAMETER (register is a parameter not a person) · B07 VERDICT (artifact: the conditioning stack) · B08 title-restate outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: I run the same pipeline for four different channels. Same script, same structure, same facts. What changes is the voice. Bear sounds like one thing. Medhavy sounds like another. This episode: how that works, and what the limit is.

props.command: same facts — make it sound like Medhavy this time

beats.narration_text: Three layers. Method: what you are — scriptwriter, explainer. Register: how you sound — Teardown cites, Wonder marvels. Brand: whose voice — Bear or Medhavy. Stack them and you get one voice. Miss a layer and the runner guesses.

props.title: the conditioning stack
props.code:
# layer 1: WHAT (method persona)
system: you are a Teardown scriptwriter

# layer 2: HOW (register)
system: cite specifics · no comfort · hard claims

# layer 3: WHOSE (brand)
system: voice: Bear / @NikBearBrown

beats.narration_text: The stack. Method persona tells the model what it is — a scriptwriter, a lecture explainer. Register tells it how to sound — Teardown: hard claims, no comfort. Wonder: the same fact through a wide lens. Brand tells it whose voice. Three conditioning layers, applied in sequence, each doing a different job.

Why this works: each layer narrows the distribution. The method biases toward certain structures. The register biases toward certain tones. The brand cues a style the model already has in its training. Every layer cuts away more of the probability space. The remaining sliver is the channel's voice.

Here is what it cannot do. Steering is not specifying. You narrow the distribution — you do not name the output. Two runs of the same prompt through the same stack can still disagree on the sentence. You have loaded the dice, not fixed the roll. That is the callback to last episode: the draw is still a draw.

The practical payoff: one pipeline, four channels. Same beat sheet, same timing script, same Remotion components. The brand key changes — that is it. The voice changes completely. Bear, Medhavy, HAI, Musinique — one writer, four costumes. The costumes are a parameter, not a rewrite.

And this is the key thing: a register is a parameter, not a person. Medhavy is not a separate author with different knowledge. Medhavy is a conditioning configuration that biases the same distribution toward wonder and warmth. The model is the same. The draw is different. One rope, different pulls.

The conditioning stack. Three layers: method, register, brand. Each narrows the distribution — none collapse it to a single answer. Steering is not specifying; the draw is still a draw. One pipeline, four channels — the brand key is the only diff. A register is a parameter, not a person. You can't name the output. You can load the dice.

artifactLines: You can't name the output. You can load the dice.
Three layers: method (what), register (how), brand (whose voice).
Each layer narrows the distribution; none collapse it to a single answer.
Steering is not specifying — the draw is still a draw.
One pipeline, four channels. The brand key is the only diff.

beats.narration_text: If this explains why Medhavy sounds different, subscribe. Nik Bear Brown.
